full_text:
Protocol for HGSOC tissue dissociation

Materials:

Laminar hood

Sterile surgical tools and scalpel

10cm tissue culture plates

Histology cassettes: located inside Färkkilä lab pipettes and falcon storage cupboard

70µm cell strainers: Färkkilä lab shelf above the scale.

50mL falcon tubes

1.5mL / 2mL Eppendorf tubes

Cryovials

Cell freezing box

Liquid nitrogen

4% Formaldehyde: 5ml of 37-41% Formaldehyde + 45 ml 1x PBS, located in the chemical cabinet next to fume hood

Collagenase and Hyaluronidase: 10x, dilute to 1x (located in Färkkilä lab -200C enzyme box)

Dispase II: stock 50U/mL, dilute to 1U/mL: 20 ul per ml (located in Färkkilä lab +40C PDL box) if you run out of this ask from Kauppi lab

Subtilsin A: stock 100mg/mL, dilute to 10mg/mL: 100 ul per ml (located in Färkkilä lab -200C enzyme box)

RQ1-RNAse free DNAs: 50 units (50 ul) per reaction (located in Färkkilä lab -200C enzyme box)

AdDF+++ medium: DMEM/F12 (500mL) + 1x Glutamax (5mL of 100x)+ 10mM HEPES (5mL 1M)+ Penstrep (5mL): Ready to use media will be in Väharautio lab cell culture fridge.

PBS+5mM Cacl2: 1xPBS (100 ml bottle) & 50mM CaCl2 (50 ml falcon tube) kept in the basket in Väharautio lab cell culture.

RBC lysis buffer: Kept in Väharautio lab cell culture fridge door, top shelf

Stem cell banker: Kept in Vähärautio lab cell culture fridge door, top shelf

Start date:

Protocol:

Sample ID:

Brief description of the sample and its appearance.

1. Dissection:

1.1. Cut the tumor tissue into three pieces (two small and one large). Snap freeze one of the small tumor piece into LN2, fix the remaining one small piece into 4% formaldehyde (in 1X PBS) overnight at 4 0C. Use the remaining bigger piece for enzymatic dissociation and single cell isolation.

2. Enzymatic dissociation:

2.1. In the laminar hood, place the big tumor piece in a 10 cm tissue culture dish and mince into smaller pieces using a sterile scalpel and or scissors

2.2. Divide the minced tumor tissue into three 3 ml tubes and perform the enzymatic digestion as follows

Tube 1: 3 ml of 1 mg/ml Collagenase (300 ul of 10x in 3 mL of media) in AdDF+++ with 50U DNase I (50 ul), incubate at @ 370C for 1 hr shake the tubes manually every 15-20 min.

Tube 2: 3 ml of 1 U/ml Dispase (60 uL of Dispase stock in 3 mL mecdia) in AdDF+++ with 50U DNase I (50 ul) incubate at @ 370C for 1 hr shake the tubes manually every 15-20 min.

Tube 3: 10 mg/ ml Subtilsin A (300 ul of stock in 3 mL of media) in PBS+5mM Cacl2+ 50U DNase I (50 ul) @ 60C (inside the cold centrifuge) shake the tubes manually every 15-20 min, incubate for 1 hr.

Note: Total volume of the dissociation varies depending on the quantity of the tumor tissue. Use 1-2 ml, or 3-5 ml for smaller and bigger tissues, respectively.

3. Filtration and centrifugation

3.1. Pre-wet 70µm strainer with cold AdDF+++ medium. Pipette the digested suspensions through the strainer into 50mL tubes and wash the strainers with 5 mL above cold medium.

3.2. Centrifuge 10 minutes @ 1000 rpm 4oC.

Note: Aliquot the medium and sterile PBS into a falcon and keep it on ice. Always use cold medium or PBS until the final step.

4. Red blood cell lysis (ONLY if there is a visible red pellet)

4.1. Upon centrifugation from step 3.2., if there is a visible red pellet, remove supernatant and resuspend the cell pellet in 2 mL RBC lysis buffer, incubate at room temperature (RT) for 5 min. Add 10 ml AdDF+++ Centrifuge 10 minutes @ 1000 rpm 4oC.

5. Resuspension of the cell pellet viabililty measurement and downstream analysis

5.1. Carefully remove the supernatant and resuspend the cell pellet in 1mL of sterile 1x PBS. Perform and cell count and determine total cell yield.

Note: Decide the volume of resuspension depending on the pellet size.

Viability analysis using Trypan Blue:

Take 10uL of cell suspension in a 1.5 ml tube add 10 ul of trypan blue mix well. Pipette into cell counting chamber. Note down the cell count & viability.

Cell count using countess

After the cell count, add 2ml of cold PBS, centrifuge at 1000 rpm for 10 min @ +4. Divide the pellet into three tubes: 1. Approx. 500000 cells for IF and viability analysis, 2. & 3. For single cell analysis (EPE) and for organoid culture (AN). Place the tubes in a cell freezing box and keep it at -80.
